verdicts.note: Score stands, but the scoring-file highlight 'past donors include AIPAC and defense contractors [S4]' miscites: S4 (FEC totals) supports the $1.13M/$420K figures, while the AIPAC/defense-contractor donor claim comes from S6 (KUOW) per the dossier. Highlight should cite S4 and S6.

The cited planks (end for-profit prisons, ban predictive policing and facial recognition) sit in the dossier's immigration/civil-liberties and privacy/AI sections, not a public-safety platform. She takes no position on policing levels, treatment, encampments, or diversion — the axis's actual tension. Mapping these planks onto the safety axis is indirect inference, which the confidence rules cap at low.

Score and direction are solid via S2, but the S1 citation is padding: the dossier attributes every foreign-policy/defense plank to S2 only; S1 (pamphlet) is cited in the dossier only for the platform's general framing. Recommend dropping S1 from this score's citations.

The 0 (genuinely mixed: decade of enacted record + explicit outsider/new-party campaign) is well calibrated. Flag: the scoring-file highlight cites the 2021 recall to [S6]; the dossier sources the recall to S4/S5, while S6 supports only the negative NPI approval polling.

Direction and magnitude are right ('shut down ICE and the detention centers' is fully pole_b), but the evidence is a single pamphlet pledge cited to one source (S1) with no record and no repetition across sources in the dossier. Per the confidence rules a single pamphlet statement is medium at best, not high — unlike her defense and healthcare scores, which are corroborated across S1/S2/S6.

The cited statement ('big government policies are making it harder to build, hire, afford, and stay') is about regulatory burden, not government spending, audits, or program expansion. Nothing in the dossier shows him calling for oversight, audits, or spending restraint — the axis's pole_a content. Reading a spending posture out of a general anti-big-government pamphlet phrase is indirect inference: low confidence, not medium.

Score stands. Flag: the scoring-file highlight cites 'reported $0 in 2026 fundraising receipts [S4]'; the dossier sources the $0 receipts to S3 (FEC), while S4 (Ballotpedia) supports the election-result percentages. Highlight should cite S3 and S4.

The sole evidence — 'hostility toward major regional employers costs jobs' [S1] — says nothing about AI, data centers, platforms, or tech regulation. The dossier contains no tech-policy position at all. Inferring an innovation-friendly regulatory posture from a generic pro-employer pamphlet line is speculation that leans on his party label rather than evidence; even at low confidence this score should not exist.

+2 (fully pole_b) is exaggerated against the cited evidence, and the scorer's own basis concedes why. Pole_b is defined first by arms embargo/aid conditions on Israel; Perasso explicitly defends 'Israel's right to exist as a refuge for Jews' and does not call for cutting aid [S1] — the dossier calls this position 'distinct from every other candidate in the race.' He also affirms defending Ukraine's sovereignty. His US-out-of-the-Middle-East and troops/nukes-out-of-Europe planks are strongly reduction-side, so +1 medium captures the mix; +2 would falsely align him with embargo-first voters.

missing.basis: The scorer skipped the social axis despite direct dossier evidence: The Stranger's endorsement write-up reported she told the board she had strategically omitted LGBTQ+ protections from her platform to avoid alienating parts of her coalition, prompting the Washington State Stonewall Democrats chair to urge organizations to reconsider her endorsements [S7]. The dossier contains no affirmative reproductive-rights or LGBTQ+ platform plank. That is a genuinely mixed signal — 0 at low confidence — and omitting it entirely hides a documented controversy on this axis.
